figures.caption: Figure 8: TabPFN-TS predictions and local SHAP feature attributions across the full test set, with one panel per month. Each panel shows: actual load (black), model predictions (blue); SHAP values indicating feature contributions to load forecasts, where positive (negative) values correspond to increased (decreased) predicted load; actual covariate values for temperature and irradiance (right y-axis), with Sundays and holidays marked in pink.

Figure 2: Dependence of Chronos-2 (left) and TabPFN-TS (right) on the holiday covariate (top), the temperature covariate (middle) and the irradiance difference to the previous day (bottom). Both TSFMs make similar use of the covariates and this use is consistent with domain knowledge. The predicted load is decreased on holidays (top), increased when the outside temperature drops on cold days (middle), and increased when PV generation drops due to lower irradiance (bottom).

Figure 7: Chronos-2 predictions and local SHAP feature attributions across the full test set, with one panel per month. Each panel shows: actual load (black), model predictions (blue); SHAP values indicating feature contributions to load forecasts, where positive (negative) values correspond to increased (decreased) predicted load; actual covariate values for temperature and irradiance (right y-axis), with Sundays and holidays marked in pink.

Figure 5: SHAP dependence plots of temperature difference between prediction day and previous day. The back line indicates points where the temperature is equal to the previous day. Bothmodels decrease the predicted load with increasing temperatures (points above the black line), and vice versa (points below the black line).

Figure 6: Local SHAP explanations for TabPFN-TS predictions on selected days showing feature contributions to load forecasts: (a) post-holiday effects (January 6-8), (b) temperature responses (February 20-21), (c) holiday patterns (May 1-2), and (d) irradiance influence (August 20). Positive (negative) SHAP values correspond to an increase (decrease) in the predicted load.
Figure 4: Performance comparison of the TabPFN-TS and Chronos-2 models across different context lengths.

Figure 3: Local SHAP explanations for Chronos-2 predictions on selected days showing feature contributions to load forecasts: (a) post-holiday effects (January 6-8), (b) temperature responses (February 20-21), (c) holiday patterns (May 1-2), and (d) irradiance influence (August 20). Positive (negative) SHAP values correspond to an increase (decrease) in the predicted load.
